prompt:
Question: Which of the following passages is the original verbatim passage? The other passages are paraphrased versions of it.

Options:
A. The anti-terrorism legislation currently under consideration by the Swedish parliament is known as the Swedish anti-terrorism bill of 2023. This proposed legal framework, which initially emerged in 2017 after the Stockholm truck attack, aims to criminalize "the participation in a terrorist organisation." Sweden has previously explored implementing anti-terrorism laws; however, these earlier attempts faced considerable resistance from human rights advocates. Although the legislation is not yet finalized, it received renewed impetus subsequent to the Russian invasion of Ukraine in 2022. Following this event, Sweden formally sought NATO membership, but its application encountered objections from Turkey. Turkey’s opposition was based on the assertion that Sweden and Finland (who submitted their applications concurrently) "host terrorist organisations which act against Turkey," specifically mentioning groups such as the PKK, PYD, YPG, and the Gülen movement. It is noteworthy that the PKK holds recognition as a terrorist organization by several international bodies.
B. The Swedish anti-terrorism bill of 2023 refers to the anti-terrorism legislation debated by the Swedish parliament. The new legislation has been first proposed in 2017 following the Stockholm truck attack and would criminalise "the participation in a terrorist organisation". Sweden has debated introducing anti-terrorism legislation before but those efforts has been hampered by human rights activists. The legislation has not been finalized but gained new momentum following the Russian invasion of Ukraine in 2022, after which Sweden applied to join NATO, only for the Swedish application to run into opposition from Turkey, on the grounds that Sweden and Finland (which also applied at the same time) "host terrorist organisations which act against Turkey", including the PKK, PYD, YPG and Gülen movement. While the PKK is recognised as a
C. The Swedish anti-terrorism bill of 2023 is the latest development in the Swedish parliament's discussion of anti-terrorism legislation. The bill was first proposed in 2017 after the Stockholm truck attack, but it was stalled by human rights activists. It gained new momentum after the Russian invasion of Ukraine in 2022, but Sweden's application to join NATO was rejected by Turkey, citing the PKK's status as a terrorist organization.

It is noteworthy that the PKK holds recognition as a terrorist organization by several international bodies.
